https://itamargilad.com/ai-pm-jobs/.summary: The article examines whether AI will eliminate Product Manager (PM) jobs by reviewing four theories. It suggests that full automation of PM work is unlikely due to its high dimensionality and complex human judgment requirements, while AI may also increase the demand for PMs to define what to build. However, the most plausible risk to PM jobs is a potential market collapse if the AI investment bubble bursts.

https://newsletter.mkt1.co/p/mutiny-grow.summary: The MKT1 newsletter offers an inside look at how Growth Marketing Lead Matt Ratchford is building Mutiny's growth engine. His updates for the AI agent for GTM teams include improving conversion with a headline change, re-orienting outbound efforts to focus on high-quality accounts, and noting that 50% of new users come from word of mouth.

https://www.interconnects.ai/p/6-months-.summary: Open-source AI models are facing a serious viability test due to new regulations being discussed at the White House. There is a likely action to ban or delay open-weights models above certain capability levels within the next six months, driven by concerns about frontier capabilities and calls to restrict Chinese open-weight models.

https://www.lennysnewsletter.com/p/how-i.summary: Claire benchmarked GPT-5.6 Sol against Claude Fable and other models for real product work, finding Sol to be the most practically effective. Sol became her new daily driver due to its ability to produce functional prototypes, easier-to-work-with writing, and practical flexibility for building products. It also excels at video editing via Codex and browser use with @Chrome for agentic workflows.

https://www.lennysnewsletter.com/p/how-t.summary: An annual Tech Worker Sentiment Survey, now in its second year, gathered responses from thousands of workers across various tech roles. The study explores how people in tech feel about their jobs, AI, burnout, and career futures, revealing that AI has split the workforce. Key findings include an 11-point jump in burnout and a sentiment that no one in tech would recommend their job to new industry entrants.

https://www.lennysnewsletter.com/p/this-.summary: Alex Finn, an AI builder, runs an ambitious local AI setup on his own hardware. His system includes three Mac Studio machines, a DGX Spark, and a custom RTX 5090 build, all coordinated through a self-built fleet dashboard. Finn has spent five months optimizing model allocation, integrating with Claude Code loops, and establishing a self-running software factory.

https://www.newcomer.co/p/ipo-party-face.summary: US IPOs have seen their biggest year since 2021, with total proceeds nearly matching the 2021 annual record. However, aftermarket performance has been disappointing, with many, including SpaceX, slumping since their debut and trading below offering prices.

https://www.newcomer.co/p/scoop-menlo-ve.summary: Menlo Ventures is seeing an Internal Rate of Return (IRR) of over 40% on its recent funds, largely driven by its substantial investment in the AI lab Anthropic. The firm committed around $1 billion to Anthropic across different vehicles, with this stake now worth $14 billion, contributing to the firm's recent success and its largest fundraise to date.

https://www.notboring.co/p/weekly-dose-o.summary: Saronic, an autonomous shipbuilding startup, saw its Corsair drone boats used in combat for the first time, striking an Iranian submarine and ship-maintenance facility. The company also announced plans for a new $3 billion shipyard, Port Alpha, in Brownsville, Texas, which is expected to create up to 10,000 jobs and build various types of ships.

https://www.saastr.com/20vc-x-saastr-thi.summary: Apple sued OpenAI this week over alleged trade secret theft involving physical prototypes and former employees, which signaled Apple's intent to make poaching expensive. The discussion also noted the start of a "token-maxing" era, driven by rapidly increasing AI spend and a shift towards two-tier model strategies for token consumption. A central, unresolved question was the total addressable market for AI coding, with concerns that leading AI companies could quickly reach the ceiling of the US developer wage market.

https://www.saastr.com/5-interesting-lea.summary: Zoom reported re-accelerating revenue growth in Q1 FY2027, reaching $1.239 billion, up 5.5% year-over-year. The company has become a "cash machine," with free cash flow margins over 40%, driven by enterprise sales and successful AI monetization. Additionally, its Anthropic stake is carried at $1.27 billion, representing a significant gain and potential additional value.

https://www.saastr.com/500m-arr-60-engin.summary: Higgsfield reached a $500M annualized revenue run rate in June, approximately 15 months after launching in March 2025, and is cash-flow positive. The company operates with about 150 people, including a core engineering team of 60, and now aggregates various AI models, with approximately 70% of its revenue coming from agencies.

https://www.saastr.com/an-hour-with-our-.summary: An AI marketing agent, 10K, completed a one-hour work session at a cost of $13.42, which is less than human minimum wages and significantly below the hourly equivalent of a human VP of Marketing. This particular cost was for a "build-and-analyze session," while the marginal cost to simply run 10K is much lower, estimated at $30-$60 per month. The low running cost is attributed to architectural choices, shifting the primary constraint from cost to the ability of teams to effectively direct AI agents.

https://www.saastr.com/claude-became-our.summary: Episode #010 of The Agents describes how Claude integrated with Replit to function as an AI VP of Product, managing feature builds. The team also used an LLM to migrate 10 years of Marketo data for $14.28 and an agent autonomously replaced a $10,000/year app in one hour.

https://www.saastr.com/dear-saastr-at-wh.summary: A startup should consider hiring an internal recruiter when consistently hiring five or more people per quarter. This approach becomes more cost-effective than paying external recruiter fees for multiple placements and allows for a better focus on culture and specific candidate profiles. External recruiters are recommended for early-stage or hard-to-fill roles before regular, high-volume hiring begins.

https://www.saastr.com/dont-put-ai-on-yo.summary: AI agents should be deployed on "B leads" rather than "A leads" for outbound sales efforts. B leads have real signal but are often ignored by human sales representatives who prioritize "A leads." Deploying AI agents on these neglected B leads can unlock significant revenue by leveraging the agents' patience to work leads humans typically skip.

https://www.saastr.com/is-software-dead-.summary: Software is not dead, but winning in the industry has become much harder due to AI, which is currently in a massive "invest mode" with spending significantly outpacing revenue. New software companies must build strong, defensible moats, such as proprietary non-public data or integration with physical sensors, to differentiate themselves from core foundation models. Founders need to understand their specific layer in the AI stack, ensure real growth, and manage compute costs efficiently.
